tasks.name: Proactive Opportunity Check
tasks.description: Runs every 3 days when project is idle — cross-dimensional scan, always suggests and asks before acting
tasks.prompt: Run a proactive opportunity check. (1) Read _brain/index.html, all files in _brain/core/, and _brain/operations/ to understand current business state. (2) Look for opportunities across ALL dimensions — not just metrics: customer contact channels available (phone, WhatsApp, email, LinkedIn) that aren't being used for follow-up; customer segments being under-served or not contacted recently; tools referenced in code or .env that could be better connected or automated; patterns in customer behavior worth acting on; processes done manually that could be scheduled; skill gaps — recurring instructions not yet captured as a permanent skill. (3) For each finding, phrase as a suggestion that asks the owner's opinion — never assume, never act unilaterally. Format: 'I noticed [observation] — would it make sense to [specific action]? What do you think?' (4) Write results to TWO places: _brain/inbox/proactive-YYYY-MM-DD.md (for next session to surface immediately) AND _brain/operations/proactive-latest.md (persistent — always overwrite with latest). (5) Update the Proactive Suggestions section in _brain/dashboard.html: replace the content between <!-- PROACTIVE_START --> and <!-- PROACTIVE_END --> with 3-5 bullet points summarizing your findings. (6) If you noticed a recurring pattern that should be a skill, create or update _brain/skills/[area].md. (7) Auto-commit: git add _brain/ && git commit -m 'brain: proactive check YYYY-MM-DD'.

tasks.name: Daily Brain Sync
tasks.description: Incremental project scan via git diff — updates brain with what changed today
tasks.prompt: Run daily brain sync: (1) Run `git diff HEAD~1 --name-only` to list all files changed or added since yesterday. (2) Read each changed file. (3) Update relevant _brain/core/ or _brain/operations/ sections based on what changed — new features, config changes, new docs, anything. (4) If new non-code files found (PDFs, notes, exports): extract knowledge and update brain. (5) Auto-commit: `git add _brain/ && git commit -m 'brain: daily sync'`.

tasks.name: Weekly Brain Review
tasks.description: Full business review — metrics, projects, customers, health check, report
tasks.prompt: Run the weekly brain review: (1) Read _brain/operations/metrics.html — compare key metrics to last week, flag any anomaly. (2) Review active projects in _brain/index.html — check their status and flag anything blocked or delayed. (3) Review _brain/skills/ — identify any gaps based on recent sessions. (4) Check _brain/scripts/queue-runner.log for recent errors — if any, surface them in the report. (5) Generate a report with: what is going well, what is anomalous, top 3 opportunities to act on this week, 1 thing to fix. Save the report to _brain/tasks/weekly-review-YYYY-MM-DD.md. Auto-commit to git.
